title: umbilicus strangulatus
intro: Umbilicus strangulatus, also known as Deadnettle, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia, characterized by its square stems and small, rounded leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Deadnettle include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs in tinctures and salves, offering a natural and effective way to address various health concerns.
